    1. graph
    2. subgraph

Annotation Interface NamedEntityGraph.Subgraph

Enclosing class:
NamedEntityGraph
@Repeatable(NamedEntityGraph.Subgraphs.class) @Target({FIELD,METHOD}) @Retention(RUNTIME) public static @interface NamedEntityGraph.Subgraph
Declares that the annotated association is the root of a subgraph of a named entity graph.
  • The graph() member specifies the name of the containing graph.
  • The subgraph() member specifies the name of a named entity graph whose root is the associated entity.

Multiple @Subgraph annotations may be applied to a single attribute, but each must specify the name of a distinct parent graph.

A Subgraph is reified at runtime as an instance of Subgraph.

Since:
4.0
See Also:
  • NamedEntityGraph
  • NamedEntityGraph.AttributeNode
  • Graph.addSubgraph(Attribute)
  • Graph.addElementSubgraph(PluralAttribute)
API note:
Alternatively, use NamedSubgraph when nesting the definition of a subgraph directly within a NamedEntityGraph annotation.

  • Optional Element Summary

    Optional Elements
    Modifier and Type
    Optional Element
    Description
    String
    graph
    The name of the containing entity graph, as specified by NamedEntityGraph.name().
    String
    subgraph
    The name of an entity graph whose root is the associated entity, as specified by NamedEntityGraph.name().

  • Element Details

    • graph

      String graph
      The name of the containing entity graph, as specified by NamedEntityGraph.name(). If no name is explicitly specified, the name defaults to the entity name of the annotated entity class.
      Default:
      ""

    • subgraph

      String subgraph
      The name of an entity graph whose root is the associated entity, as specified by NamedEntityGraph.name(). If no subgraph name is explicitly specified, the subgraph name defaults to the name of the containing graph.

      The target subgraph must be explicitly declared via a NamedEntityGraph annotation of the target entity of the annotated association.

      Default:
      ""
